response: The three main psychedelic compounds found in Psilocybin genus mushrooms that are converted to psilocin by the body are psilocybin, psilocin, and baeocystin. Psilocybin is quickly converted by the body to psilocin, which has mind-altering effects similar to those of LSD, mescaline, and DMT. Baeocystin is an "N"-demethylated derivative of psilocybin and a phosphorylated derivative of 4-HO-NMT, but it is not converted to psilocin by the body. Therefore, only psilocybin is converted to psilocin by the body.
